paper_title: Earth System Model COSMOS Application for Miocene Climate Study

methods.description: Setup of the fully coupled Earth System Model COSMOS for simulating climate conditions during the early to middle Miocene period, including adjustments for orography, paleobathymetry, and ice sheets.

steps.description: Apply the fully coupled Earth System Model COSMOS, which includes the atmosphere model ECHAM5, the land-vegetation model JSBACH, and the ocean model MPI-OM.
steps.searchable_summary: initialize Earth System Model COSMOS with ECHAM5, JSBACH, and MPI-OM

steps.description: Set the atmosphere model at T31 spherical horizontal resolution (~3.75°× 3.75°) with 19 vertical layers.
steps.searchable_summary: configure atmosphere model resolution and layers

steps.description: Run the land-vegetation model at the same horizontal resolution as the atmosphere model.
Resolve the ocean model at 40 uneven vertical layers with a resolution of GR30 (3°× 1.8°).
steps.searchable_summary: configure ocean model resolution and layers

steps.description: Implement a regional high-resolution bathymetric reconstruction comprising the North Atlantic/Arctic Ocean.
Refine the model setup by including a more realistic regional Weddell Sea bathymetry reconstruction.
Transfer ocean grid cells representing the Barents Sea region to land cells.
steps.searchable_summary: convert Barents Sea ocean cells to land cells

methods.description: Simulation of climate conditions during the Miocene using the configured Earth System Model and analysis of the results.

steps.description: Prescribe an atmospheric CO2 level of 450 ppm for the reference simulation (MIO_450).
steps.searchable_summary: set atmospheric CO2 level for simulation

steps.description: Integrate the model for 4 kyr to minimize temperature/salinity trends in the deep ocean.
Perform ocean gateway sensitivity experiments at various height dimensions of the GSR and FS.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct sensitivity experiments on ocean gateways

steps.description: Use a climatological period of the final 100 yr of model simulation for analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze final 100 years of simulation data
